entries.title: Carboplatin De-escalation
entries.subtitle: neoCARHP · Breast cancer · Early HER2-positive
entries.summary: Similar pathologic complete response while omitting carboplatin. Approximately 10 fewer grade 3-4 neutropenia events and 9 fewer severe leukopenia events per 100 patients.
entries.headline: 64.1% vs 65.9% pCR · Noninferior response with lower severe hematologic toxicity
entries.searchText: carboplatin de-escalation neocarhp breast cancer early her2-positive roche + generic manufacturers phase iii high, pending survival 64.1% vs 65.9% pcr noninferior response with lower severe hematologic toxicity similar pathologic complete response while omitting carboplatin. approximately 10 fewer grade 3-4 neutropenia events and 9 fewer severe leukopenia events per 100 patients. lower neutropenia, leukopenia, thrombocytopenia, anemia, nausea and treatment interruption. long-term event-free and overall survival remain immature.
sections: 64.1% vs 65.9% pCR. Noninferior response with lower severe hematologic toxicity
Similar pathologic complete response while omitting carboplatin. Approximately 10 fewer grade 3-4 neutropenia events and 9 fewer severe leukopenia events per 100 patients.
Lower neutropenia, leukopenia, thrombocytopenia, anemia, nausea and treatment interruption. Long-term event-free and overall survival remain immature.

Cost-saving strategy. Components available: carboplatin ₹1,900-₹2,800; trastuzumab biosimilars ₹17,700-₹57,500; Perjeta ₹2.52-₹2.62 lakh per vial. Treatment approach still requires guideline and clinician adoption.
No new product launch needed. Available
pCR is a surrogate. Survival follow-up and high-risk subgroup selection are still required.

entries.title: Tucatinib + HER2 Maintenance
entries.subtitle: Tucatinib · Breast cancer · Metastatic HER2-positive
entries.summary: A direct randomized improvement over trastuzumab/pertuzumab maintenance, with a 35.9% relative reduction in progression or death. Benefit appeared across hormone-receptor and brain-metastasis subgroups.
entries.headline: +8.6 months median PFS · 24.9 vs 16.3 months; HR 0.641
entries.searchText: tucatinib + her2 maintenance tucatinib breast cancer metastatic her2-positive pfizer / seagen; indian generics phase iii high +8.6 months median pfs 24.9 vs 16.3 months; hr 0.641 a direct randomized improvement over trastuzumab/pertuzumab maintenance, with a 35.9% relative reduction in progression or death. benefit appeared across hormone-receptor and brain-metastasis subgroups. diarrhea in 72.7%, grade 3 or higher in 6.1%. grade 3 or higher alt and ast elevations were 13.5% and 7.1%; 13.5% discontinued for treatment-emergent toxicity. tukysa perjeta
sections: +8.6 months median PFS. 24.9 vs 16.3 months; HR 0.641

A direct randomized improvement over trastuzumab/pertuzumab maintenance, with a 35.9% relative reduction in progression or death. Benefit appeared across hormone-receptor and brain-metastasis subgroups.
Diarrhea in 72.7%, grade 3 or higher in 6.1%. Grade 3 or higher ALT and AST elevations were 13.5% and 7.1%; 13.5% discontinued for treatment-emergent toxicity.

High added continuous cost. ₹6,000 retail or ₹7,678-₹8,190 MRP per 10 × 150 mg tablets; about ₹72,000-₹98,000 per 30 days at 300 mg twice daily. Indian approval is for previously treated disease with trastuzumab and capecitabine, not HER2CLIMB-05 first-line maintenance.
Launched after 8 April 2025 approval. Approved and marketed
Overall survival is immature; long-term value depends on CNS-event avoidance, treatment duration and net pricing.

entries.title: Palbociclib HER2 Maintenance
entries.subtitle: Palbociclib · Breast cancer · Metastatic HR-positive / HER2-positive
entries.summary: Adds CDK4/6 inhibition to trastuzumab-based endocrine maintenance after induction therapy, delaying progression versus maintenance without palbociclib in HR-positive, HER2-positive advanced disease.
entries.headline: PFS HR 0.76 · PATINA maintenance approval after induction therapy
entries.searchText: palbociclib her2 maintenance palbociclib breast cancer metastatic hr-positive / her2-positive pfizer; trastuzumab/pertuzumab manufacturers phase iii high pfs hr 0.76 patina maintenance approval after induction therapy adds cdk4/6 inhibition to trastuzumab-based endocrine maintenance after induction therapy, delaying progression versus maintenance without palbociclib in hr-positive, her2-positive advanced disease. fda highlights neutropenia and interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis warnings for palbociclib. overall survival was not mature at the approval analysis, and median pfs could not be adequately described because of censoring. ibrance perjeta
sections: PFS HR 0.76. PATINA maintenance approval after induction therapy
Adds CDK4/6 inhibition to trastuzumab-based endocrine maintenance after induction therapy, delaying progression versus maintenance without palbociclib in HR-positive, HER2-positive advanced disease.

FDA highlights neutropenia and interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis warnings for palbociclib. Overall survival was not mature at the approval analysis, and median PFS could not be adequately described because of censoring.

High ongoing oral targeted-therapy cost. No verified official India MRP update was captured in this pass. Palbociclib is already marketed in India, but CDSCO approval for this HER2-positive maintenance regimen was not verified.
US HR-positive/HER2-positive maintenance indication added 24 June 2026. Product marketed
Investigator-assessed PFS improved, but OS is immature and the FDA review notes censoring limits simple median-PFS interpretation.

entries.title: Gedatolisib + Fulvestrant
entries.subtitle: Revtorpyk · Breast cancer · HR-positive / HER2-negative after endocrine progression
entries.summary: FDA approved gedatolisib with fulvestrant, with or without palbociclib, for PIK3CA-wild-type HR-positive/HER2-negative metastatic breast cancer after endocrine progression, adding a large progression-free-survival gain in a setting where many patients have already exhausted first-line endocrine options.
entries.headline: PFS HR 0.24-0.33 · 9.3 vs 2.0 months or 7.4 vs 2.0 in VIKTORIA-1
entries.searchText: gedatolisib + fulvestrant revtorpyk breast cancer hr-positive / her2-negative after endocrine progression celcuity phase iii high pfs hr 0.24-0.33 9.3 vs 2.0 months or 7.4 vs 2.0 in viktoria-1 fda approved gedatolisib with fulvestrant, with or without palbociclib, for pik3ca-wild-type hr-positive/her2-negative metastatic breast cancer after endocrine progression, adding a large progression-free-survival gain in a setting where many patients have already exhausted first-line endocrine options. the label carries warnings for stomatitis, dermatologic adverse reactions, hyperglycemia, and embryo-fetal toxicity. overall survival data were immature at the approval analysis. revtorpyk ibrance
sections: Breast cancer · HR-positive / HER2-negative after endocrine progression

392 adults with locally advanced inoperable or metastatic HR-positive, HER2-negative breast cancer without a detected PIK3CA mutation following progression on or after at least one line of endocrine therapy in the metastatic setting
Gedatolisib plus fulvestrant plus palbociclib, or gedatolisib plus fulvestrant, versus fulvestrant alone in the open-label randomized VIKTORIA-1 trial
Blinded independent central review progression-free survival for each gedatolisib arm versus fulvestrant alone; OS, ORR, and duration of response were additional endpoints

Median PFS was 9.3 versus 2.0 months for the triplet arm (HR 0.24; 95% CI 0.17-0.35) and 7.4 versus 2.0 months for the doublet arm (HR 0.33; 95% CI 0.24-0.48). ORR was 32%, 28%, and 1%, respectively; median duration of response was 17.5 months and 12.0 months in the gedatolisib arms.
FDA approved on 14 July 2026. No verified EMA, CDSCO, or other national authorization for gedatolisib was captured in this pass.
PFS HR 0.24-0.33. 9.3 vs 2.0 months or 7.4 vs 2.0 in VIKTORIA-1

FDA approved gedatolisib with fulvestrant, with or without palbociclib, for PIK3CA-wild-type HR-positive/HER2-negative metastatic breast cancer after endocrine progression, adding a large progression-free-survival gain in a setting where many patients have already exhausted first-line endocrine options.
The label carries warnings for stomatitis, dermatologic adverse reactions, hyperglycemia, and embryo-fetal toxicity. Overall survival data were immature at the approval analysis.

New intravenous targeted-therapy cost not yet officially posted. No official US list price, India price, or government procurement figure for Revtorpyk was verified in this pass. No verified CDSCO approval, India launch date, or official reimbursement/procurement announcement for gedatolisib was identified.
FDA approval 14 July 2026. US approved; India launch unverified

The pivotal trial was open-label, overall survival was immature with 25% deaths at the PFS analysis, and the approved label applies only to tumors without a detected PIK3CA mutation after prior metastatic endocrine therapy.

entries.title: Tumor-Agnostic Selpercatinib
entries.subtitle: Retevmo · RET fusion solid tumors · Locally advanced or metastatic, post-systemic therapy
entries.snapshot: Basket trial / registrational · High in biomarker-defined rare cancers
entries.summary: FDA converted selpercatinib's tumor-agnostic RET fusion indication to traditional approval, preserving a genomics-first option across multiple rare solid tumors where matched targeted therapies are uncommon and biopsy-quality molecular testing directly changes treatment choice.
entries.headline: ORR 47% · Median DoR 24.5 months in LIBRETTO-001
entries.statusLabel: US approved; India solid-tumor label unverified
badges: US approved; India solid-tumor label unverified
entries.searchText: tumor-agnostic selpercatinib retevmo ret fusion solid tumors locally advanced or metastatic, post-systemic therapy eli lilly and company basket trial / registrational high in biomarker-defined rare cancers orr 47% median dor 24.5 months in libretto-001 fda converted selpercatinib's tumor-agnostic ret fusion indication to traditional approval, preserving a genomics-first option across multiple rare solid tumors where matched targeted therapies are uncommon and biopsy-quality molecular testing directly changes treatment choice. the label warns of hepatotoxicity, interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis, hypertension, qt prolongation, hemorrhagic events, hypersensitivity, tumor lysis syndrome, impaired wound healing, hypothyroidism, embryo-fetal toxicity, and pediatric slipped capital femoral epiphysis. retevmo retsevmo
sections: RET fusion solid tumors · Locally advanced or metastatic, post-systemic therapy
14 July 2026 traditional FDA approval · United States

75 adults with RET fusion-positive solid tumors other than NSCLC and thyroid cancer in LIBRETTO-001, with pediatric and young-adult support from LIBRETTO-121 in refractory RET fusion-positive solid tumors
Single-arm basket-trial evidence with no randomized control arm; activity was judged by response rate and response durability with additional support from prior RET-driven lung and thyroid cohorts
Overall response rate and duration of response

ORR was 47% (95% CI 35-59) with a median duration of response of 24.5 months (95% CI 11.2-49.1). Adult responses were seen across colorectal, pancreatic, salivary, soft tissue sarcoma, cholangiocarcinoma, skin, unknown-primary, breast, bronchial carcinoid, ovarian, small-intestine, and neuroendocrine tumors.

FDA granted traditional approval on 14 July 2026 after earlier accelerated approvals in adults and pediatric patients. No verified EMA or CDSCO tumor-agnostic RET-fusion authorization beyond this FDA action was captured in this pass.
ORR 47%. Median DoR 24.5 months in LIBRETTO-001

FDA converted selpercatinib's tumor-agnostic RET fusion indication to traditional approval, preserving a genomics-first option across multiple rare solid tumors where matched targeted therapies are uncommon and biopsy-quality molecular testing directly changes treatment choice.

The label warns of hepatotoxicity, interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis, hypertension, QT prolongation, hemorrhagic events, hypersensitivity, tumor lysis syndrome, impaired wound healing, hypothyroidism, embryo-fetal toxicity, and pediatric slipped capital femoral epiphysis.

Precision targeted-therapy cost remains high. No official India price, procurement figure, or tumor-agnostic RET-fusion label update for selpercatinib was verified in this pass. No verified CDSCO authorization, India launch update, or reimbursement/procurement announcement for this tumor-agnostic RET-fusion use was identified.
Traditional FDA approval 14 July 2026. US approved; India solid-tumor label unverified

The registrational evidence was single-arm and tumor-agnostic rather than randomized, with heterogeneous histologies and relatively sparse pediatric non-thyroid data, so effect size by tumor type and sequencing versus local standards still need case-by-case judgment.

entries.title: Perioperative Pembrolizumab + Enfortumab
entries.subtitle: Padcev + Keytruda · Bladder cancer · Muscle-invasive, cystectomy-eligible
entries.summary: FDA expanded perioperative pembrolizumab plus enfortumab vedotin to all cystectomy-eligible muscle-invasive bladder cancer patients, not just the cisplatin-ineligible subset, after a direct randomized improvement over gemcitabine/cisplatin in a curative-intent setting.
entries.headline: EFS HR 0.53 · OS HR 0.65 in KEYNOTE-B15 / EV-304
entries.statusLabel: US approved; India perioperative label unverified
badges: US approved; India perioperative label unverified
entries.searchText: perioperative pembrolizumab + enfortumab padcev + keytruda bladder cancer muscle-invasive, cystectomy-eligible merck + astellas pharma phase iii high efs hr 0.53 os hr 0.65 in keynote-b15 / ev-304 fda expanded perioperative pembrolizumab plus enfortumab vedotin to all cystectomy-eligible muscle-invasive bladder cancer patients, not just the cisplatin-ineligible subset, after a direct randomized improvement over gemcitabine/cisplatin in a curative-intent setting. pembrolizumab retains immune-mediated and infusion-related toxicity risks. enfortumab vedotin carries warnings for skin reactions, hyperglycemia, pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease, peripheral neuropathy, ocular disorders and infusion site extravasation. keytruda
sections: 10 July 2026 FDA approval · United States; global Project Orbis review with other agencies still ongoing
808 previously untreated adults with muscle-invasive bladder cancer who were candidates for radical cystectomy with pelvic lymph node dissection and were eligible for cisplatin-based chemotherapy
Neoadjuvant pembrolizumab plus enfortumab vedotin followed by surgery and adjuvant pembrolizumab plus enfortumab vedotin versus neoadjuvant gemcitabine and cisplatin followed by surgery
Event-free survival by blinded independent central review; overall survival was an additional efficacy endpoint
Median EFS was not reached versus 48.5 months; HR 0.53 (95% CI 0.41-0.70). Median OS was not reached in either arm; HR 0.65 (95% CI 0.48-0.89).

FDA approved on priority review and five weeks ahead of the goal date. The FDA page states collaborating Project Orbis agency reviews remain ongoing; no verified CDSCO, EMA or other national authorization for this perioperative use was captured in this pass.
EFS HR 0.53. OS HR 0.65 in KEYNOTE-B15 / EV-304

FDA expanded perioperative pembrolizumab plus enfortumab vedotin to all cystectomy-eligible muscle-invasive bladder cancer patients, not just the cisplatin-ineligible subset, after a direct randomized improvement over gemcitabine/cisplatin in a curative-intent setting.

Pembrolizumab retains immune-mediated and infusion-related toxicity risks. Enfortumab vedotin carries warnings for skin reactions, hyperglycemia, pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease, peripheral neuropathy, ocular disorders and infusion site extravasation.

High dual-agent perioperative immunotherapy plus ADC cost. No official US list-price update, India price, or government procurement figure for this perioperative regimen was verified in this pass. No verified CDSCO approval, India launch date, or official reimbursement/procurement announcement for this muscle-invasive bladder cancer use was identified.
FDA approval 10 July 2026. US approved; India perioperative label unverified

The pivotal trial was open-label and enrolled cisplatin-eligible surgical candidates. Longer follow-up is still needed for cure durability, perioperative tolerability in routine practice, and regional reimbursement/adoption outside the FDA setting.

entries.title: Subcutaneous Isatuximab
entries.subtitle: Sarclisa Escena · Multiple myeloma · Newly diagnosed + relapsed / refractory
entries.snapshot: Noninferiority / phase II · Operationally high, clinical impact unproven
entries.summary: FDA approved subcutaneous isatuximab across three multiple myeloma regimens, a material care-delivery shift that could reduce chair-time burden and make anti-CD38 treatment logistics easier without losing short-term activity versus IV delivery in the pivotal noninferiority trial.
entries.headline: ORR 71.1% vs 70.5% · Subcutaneous route matched IV activity in IRAKLIA
entries.searchText: subcutaneous isatuximab sarclisa escena multiple myeloma newly diagnosed + relapsed / refractory sanofi noninferiority / phase ii operationally high, clinical impact unproven orr 71.1% vs 70.5% subcutaneous route matched iv activity in iraklia fda approved subcutaneous isatuximab across three multiple myeloma regimens, a material care-delivery shift that could reduce chair-time burden and make anti-cd38 treatment logistics easier without losing short-term activity versus iv delivery in the pivotal noninferiority trial. label warnings include hypersensitivity and other administration reactions, neutropenia, infections, secondary primary malignancies, laboratory test interference and embryo-fetal toxicity.
sections: Multiple myeloma · Newly diagnosed + relapsed / refractory

IRAKLIA randomized 531 previously treated patients; supporting single-arm phase II datasets enrolled 74 relapsed/refractory patients in IZALCO and 74 transplant-ineligible newly diagnosed patients in IsaSocut
Subcutaneous isatuximab with pomalidomide/dexamethasone versus intravenous isatuximab with pomalidomide/dexamethasone in IRAKLIA; single-arm support for the carfilzomib/dexamethasone and VRd regimens
Overall response rate and steady-state trough concentration in IRAKLIA

IRAKLIA ORR was 71.1% (95% CI 65.2-76.5) with subcutaneous dosing versus 70.5% (95% CI 64.7-75.9) with IV dosing; steady-state trough geometric mean ratio was 1.53 (90% CI 1.32-1.78). Supporting ORR was 79.7% (95% CI 68.8-88.2) in IZALCO and 97.3% (95% CI 90.6-99.7) in IsaSocut.
FDA approved subcutaneous isatuximab-irfc across three multiple myeloma indications. No verified EMA, CDSCO or other national approval for the subcutaneous product was captured in this pass.
ORR 71.1% vs 70.5%. Subcutaneous route matched IV activity in IRAKLIA

FDA approved subcutaneous isatuximab across three multiple myeloma regimens, a material care-delivery shift that could reduce chair-time burden and make anti-CD38 treatment logistics easier without losing short-term activity versus IV delivery in the pivotal noninferiority trial.
Label warnings include hypersensitivity and other administration reactions, neutropenia, infections, secondary primary malignancies, laboratory test interference and embryo-fetal toxicity.

Route-convenience value, but official pricing not verified. No official US list-price update, India price, or procurement figure for Sarclisa Escena was verified in this pass. No verified CDSCO approval, India launch announcement, or local delivery-system availability was identified for the subcutaneous product.
FDA approval 9 July 2026. US approved; India route unverified

The pivotal evidence established noninferior response and higher trough concentrations versus IV delivery, not superior survival or quality-of-life outcomes. Two of the three approved regimens relied on 74-patient phase II datasets rather than randomized route-comparison trials.

entries.title: Tregzi Transplant Cell Therapy
entries.subtitle: Tregzi · Hematologic malignancies · Matched-donor myeloablative HSCT
entries.summary: FDA approved a personalized allogeneic Treg/HSPC/T-cell graft platform that reduced chronic GVHD or death after matched-donor transplant in adults with acute leukemias or MDS, addressing a major toxicity bottleneck in curative-intent blood-cancer care.
entries.headline: cGVHD-free survival HR 0.26 · 12-month cGFS 78% vs 38% in Precision-T
entries.searchText: tregzi transplant cell therapy tregzi hematologic malignancies matched-donor myeloablative hsct orca bio phase iii high cgvhd-free survival hr 0.26 12-month cgfs 78% vs 38% in precision-t fda approved a personalized allogeneic treg/hspc/t-cell graft platform that reduced chronic gvhd or death after matched-donor transplant in adults with acute leukemias or mds, addressing a major toxicity bottleneck in curative-intent blood-cancer care. common adverse reactions included mucositis, diarrhea, rash and infections. the label warns of graft failure, acute and chronic gvhd, infusion reactions, secondary malignancies, donor-origin malignancies and infectious-agent transmission. tregzi orca-t
sections: 30 June 2026 FDA approval · United States; multicenter US trial
187 adults aged 19-65 years with AML, ALL, MDS or MPAL undergoing matched-donor allogeneic HSCT after myeloablative conditioning
Unmanipulated allograft with tacrolimus plus methotrexate GVHD prophylaxis versus Tregzi followed by tacrolimus alone
Chronic GVHD-free survival, defined as death or moderate-to-severe chronic GVHD after transplant
Median cGFS was not estimable versus 7.3 months; HR 0.26 (95% CI 0.14-0.47). Moderate-to-severe chronic GVHD at 12 months was 12.6% versus 44.0%; HR 0.19 (95% CI 0.08-0.43).
FDA approved with priority review, orphan drug designation and RMAT designation. No verified EMA, CDSCO or other national approval was captured in this pass.
cGVHD-free survival HR 0.26. 12-month cGFS 78% vs 38% in Precision-T

FDA approved a personalized allogeneic Treg/HSPC/T-cell graft platform that reduced chronic GVHD or death after matched-donor transplant in adults with acute leukemias or MDS, addressing a major toxicity bottleneck in curative-intent blood-cancer care.

Common adverse reactions included mucositis, diarrhea, rash and infections. The label warns of graft failure, acute and chronic GVHD, infusion reactions, secondary malignancies, donor-origin malignancies and infectious-agent transmission.

Personalized transplant cell therapy; official price not verified. No official US list price, India price, or public procurement figure was verified in this pass. No verified CDSCO filing, India launch date, or transplant-center availability announcement was identified.
FDA approval 30 June 2026. US approved; India unverified

Open-label transplant evidence in matched-donor myeloablative HSCT needs longer follow-up for relapse, late survival, generalizability beyond trial centers and eventual affordability/access assessment.

entries.title: Neoadjuvant Durvalumab
entries.subtitle: Durvalumab · Breast cancer · Early triple-negative
entries.summary: Improved invasive disease-free, distant disease-free and overall survival despite no decisive overall pCR improvement. Suggests durable immune priming may be possible without routine adjuvant continuation.
entries.headline: OS HR 0.33 · Long-term follow-up at median 86.4 months
entries.searchText: neoadjuvant durvalumab durvalumab breast cancer early triple-negative astrazeneca phase ii moderate-high os hr 0.33 long-term follow-up at median 86.4 months improved invasive disease-free, distant disease-free and overall survival despite no decisive overall pcr improvement. suggests durable immune priming may be possible without routine adjuvant continuation. checkpoint blockade can cause permanent endocrinopathies and serious immune pneumonitis, hepatitis, colitis and other organ toxicity. imfinzi
sections: OS HR 0.33. Long-term follow-up at median 86.4 months

Improved invasive disease-free, distant disease-free and overall survival despite no decisive overall pCR improvement. Suggests durable immune priming may be possible without routine adjuvant continuation.
Checkpoint blockade can cause permanent endocrinopathies and serious immune pneumonitis, hepatitis, colitis and other organ toxicity.

High; shorter duration could save cost. Imfinzi 500 mg revised MRP ₹1.713 lakh; pharmacy listings approximately ₹1.47-₹1.71 lakh. The GeparNuevo early-TNBC use is not an established Indian labeled indication.
Small phase II trial and no direct comparison with current perioperative pembrolizumab standard.

entries.title: Personalized mRNA Neoantigen Therapy
entries.subtitle: Intismeran · Melanoma · Resected high-risk
entries.summary: A large and durable recurrence-free survival signal from patient-specific tumor neoantigens. Five-year distant metastasis-free survival also favored combination treatment.
entries.headline: +19.7 points 5-year RFS · 68.8% vs 49.1%; RFS HR 0.51
entries.searchText: personalized mrna neoantigen therapy intismeran melanoma resected high-risk moderna + merck / msd phase iib potentially transformative +19.7 points 5-year rfs 68.8% vs 49.1%; rfs hr 0.51 a large and durable recurrence-free survival signal from patient-specific tumor neoantigens. five-year distant metastasis-free survival also favored combination treatment. no new signal reported; injection-related symptoms and pembrolizumab immune toxicity remain relevant. tissue adequacy and manufacturing delay add nonmedical burden. keytruda mrna-4157 v940
sections: A large and durable recurrence-free survival signal from patient-specific tumor neoantigens. Five-year distant metastasis-free survival also favored combination treatment.
No new signal reported; injection-related symptoms and pembrolizumab immune toxicity remain relevant. Tissue adequacy and manufacturing delay add nonmedical burden.
Unknown; likely very high initially. No commercial price. Requires positive phase III results, global filing, individualized manufacturing validation and CDSCO review.
No announced date; late-2020s at earliest is an inference. Not launched
Phase IIb sample size, small control group and nondefinitive overall survival. Phase III confirmation is ongoing.

entries.title: First-line Pemigatinib
entries.subtitle: Pemigatinib · Cholangiocarcinoma · FGFR2-rearranged
entries.snapshot: Phase III · High in biomarker subset
entries.summary: Improved response and progression-free survival over chemotherapy in a molecularly selected population. The result supports early broad genomic testing.
entries.headline: ORR 47% vs 15% · PFS 8.3 vs 6.8 months
entries.searchText: first-line pemigatinib pemigatinib cholangiocarcinoma fgfr2-rearranged incyte phase iii high in biomarker subset orr 47% vs 15% pfs 8.3 vs 6.8 months improved response and progression-free survival over chemotherapy in a molecularly selected population. the result supports early broad genomic testing. fgfr inhibition requires monitoring for hyperphosphatemia, ocular toxicity, nail and skin changes, stomatitis and dose interruption. pemazyre
sections: Improved response and progression-free survival over chemotherapy in a molecularly selected population. The result supports early broad genomic testing.
FGFR inhibition requires monitoring for hyperphosphatemia, ocular toxicity, nail and skin changes, stomatitis and dose interruption.

Very high. No verified Indian MRP. US $20,286 per 14 tablets converts to about ₹19.4 lakh, or about ₹3.34 crore annually; not an Indian selling price. Named-patient import may be possible; first-line positioning requires indication-specific approval.
No public India date. No verified domestic launch
Applies to a small FGFR2-rearranged subset and depends on timely, high-quality molecular testing.

entries.title: Amivantamab in Refractory CRC
entries.subtitle: Amivantamab · Colorectal cancer · RAS/BRAF wild-type
entries.summary: Activity was notable after prior anti-EGFR therapy and in right-sided disease, where conventional EGFR antibodies can be less reliable.
entries.headline: ORR 19%–29% · Median PFS 3.7–5.7 months
entries.searchText: amivantamab in refractory crc amivantamab colorectal cancer ras/braf wild-type johnson & johnson / janssen phase ib/ii promising, not practice-changing orr 19%–29% median pfs 3.7–5.7 months activity was notable after prior anti-egfr therapy and in right-sided disease, where conventional egfr antibodies can be less reliable. grade 3 or higher rash 7%, acneiform dermatitis 4% and hypoalbuminemia 4%; infusion reactions and skin toxicity add operational burden. rybrevant
sections: Activity was notable after prior anti-EGFR therapy and in right-sided disease, where conventional EGFR antibodies can be less reliable.
Grade 3 or higher rash 7%, acneiform dermatitis 4% and hypoalbuminemia 4%; infusion reactions and skin toxicity add operational burden.
Very high. US $3,894 per 350 mg vial converts to about ₹3.72 lakh per vial; not an Indian market price. Rybrevant approvals in other tumor settings do not establish colorectal-cancer use.
No public India date for colorectal cancer. Trial / import context
Small nonrandomized cohorts, biomarker-intensive selection, no survival comparison and high acquisition cost.

entries.title: Integrin Beta-6 ADC
entries.subtitle: Sigvotatug · Lung cancer · Advanced NSCLC
entries.summary: The taxane-naive nonsquamous subgroup signal appears favorable to historical docetaxel expectations, with long response duration among responders.
entries.headline: ORR 29% in subgroup · PFS 6.4 months; response duration 12.8 months
entries.searchText: integrin beta-6 adc sigvotatug lung cancer advanced nsclc pfizer / seagen phase i promising orr 29% in subgroup pfs 6.4 months; response duration 12.8 months the taxane-naive nonsquamous subgroup signal appears favorable to historical docetaxel expectations, with long response duration among responders. peripheral sensory neuropathy is a key cumulative toxicity. at the selected schedule, 19% discontinued for treatment-emergent events.
sections: ORR 29% in subgroup. PFS 6.4 months; response duration 12.8 months
The taxane-naive nonsquamous subgroup signal appears favorable to historical docetaxel expectations, with long response duration among responders.
Peripheral sensory neuropathy is a key cumulative toxicity. At the selected schedule, 19% discontinued for treatment-emergent events.
Unknown; likely high ADC cost. No commercial price. Phase III efficacy, safety and subsequent global and CDSCO approvals are required.
No announced date; late-2020s at earliest is an inference. Not launched
First-in-human, heterogeneous dosing and populations, historical comparator and no validated routine selection biomarker.

entries.title: Neoadjuvant Sacituzumab Govitecan
entries.subtitle: Sacituzumab · Bladder cancer · Cisplatin-ineligible MIBC
entries.summary: Provides proof that TROP2-directed therapy is active in muscle-invasive bladder cancer and may support future subtype-guided ADC selection.
entries.headline: ypT0N0-x 29.5% · 24-month event-free survival 71.4%
entries.statusLabel: Imported product; study use not launched
badges: Imported product; study use not launched
entries.searchText: neoadjuvant sacituzumab govitecan sacituzumab bladder cancer cisplatin-ineligible mibc gilead sciences phase ii exploratory ypt0n0-x 29.5% 24-month event-free survival 71.4% provides proof that trop2-directed therapy is active in muscle-invasive bladder cancer and may support future subtype-guided adc selection. neutropenia, diarrhea and infection are central concerns. two early deaths led to dose reduction and prophylaxis; one death was treatment-related. trodelvy
sections: Provides proof that TROP2-directed therapy is active in muscle-invasive bladder cancer and may support future subtype-guided ADC selection.
Neutropenia, diarrhea and infection are central concerns. Two early deaths led to dose reduction and prophylaxis; one death was treatment-related.
High. Import listings approximately ₹1.85-₹1.90 lakh per 180–200 mg vial; supplier quotes, not official MRP. The SURE-01 regimen needs comparative confirmation and regulatory review.
No public India date for this indication. Imported product; study use not launched
Small single-arm evidence, protocol changes, incomplete cystectomy assessment and a stronger contemporary benchmark.

entries.title: Durvalumab + FLOT
entries.subtitle: MATTERHORN · Gastric / GEJ cancer
entries.summary: The ASCO signal completed the conference-to-label pathway within six months. FDA authorized perioperative durvalumab with FLOT followed by durvalumab for resectable gastric or GEJ adenocarcinoma.
entries.headline: At FDA review: median EFS not reached vs 32.8 months; HR 0.71 (95% CI 0.58–0.86). OS HR 0.78 (95% CI 0.63–0.96). pCR 19.2% vs 7.2%.

sections: At FDA review: median EFS not reached vs 32.8 months; HR 0.71 (95% CI 0.58–0.86). OS HR 0.78 (95% CI 0.63–0.96). pCR 19.2% vs 7.2%.

entries.title: Galleri-NHS negative screening signal

entries.summary: This is a major cautionary signal for population blood-based multi-cancer screening. A negative primary endpoint should slow premature adoption and reshape how MCED claims are presented to payers and the public.

entries.title: WHO/IARC cancer inequity report
entries.subtitle: Public health / equity · All cancers · Global
entries.organization: World Health Organization + International Agency for Research on Cancer
entries.summary: This is a direct cancer-care delivery and equity signal rather than a drug-development headline. It quantifies how much avoidable cancer harm now depends on diagnosis pathways, UHC design, workforce, medicine access and social protection rather than on scientific novelty alone.
entries.headline: WHO reported 20.6 million new cases and close to 10 million deaths annually, with annual cases projected to rise to nearly 35 million by 2050. Fewer than one in three countries currently include cancer care in universal health coverage packages, availability of the top 20 priority cancer medicines ranges from 9%-54% in low- and lower-middle-income countries versus 68%-94% in high-income countries, and at least 45% of affected people report financial hardship. WHO's linked breast-cancer survival study estimated median 5-year survival at 41.9% in low-income countries versus 87.3% in high-income countries.
entries.searchText: who/iarc cancer inequity report who 2026 report public health / equity all cancers global world health organization + international agency for research on cancer who and iarc quantify widening cancer-control, survival and access gaps who reported 20.6 million new cases and close to 10 million deaths annually, with annual cases projected to rise to nearly 35 million by 2050. fewer than one in three countries currently include cancer care in universal health coverage packages, availability of the top 20 priority cancer medicines ranges from 9%-54% in low- and lower-middle-income countries versus 68%-94% in high-income countries, and at least 45% of affected people report financial hardship. who's linked breast-cancer survival study estimated median 5-year survival at 41.9% in low-income countries versus 87.3% in high-income countries. whether countries accelerate cancer inclusion in uhc packages, strengthen prevention and early diagnosis, expand essential-medicine access, and incorporate patient and caregiver burden into cancer policy. this is a direct cancer-care delivery and equity signal rather than a drug-development headline. it quantifies how much avoidable cancer harm now depends on diagnosis pathways, uhc design, workforce, medicine access and social protection rather than on scientific novelty alone.
sections: WHO and IARC quantify widening cancer-control, survival and access gaps
People affected by cancer, caregivers, and national cancer systems across 194 WHO Member States
Current 2024-2026 global burden and access baseline vs 2050 projections; low-income vs high-income survival and medicine-access gaps

WHO reported 20.6 million new cases and close to 10 million deaths annually, with annual cases projected to rise to nearly 35 million by 2050. Fewer than one in three countries currently include cancer care in universal health coverage packages, availability of the top 20 priority cancer medicines ranges from 9%-54% in low- and lower-middle-income countries versus 68%-94% in high-income countries, and at least 45% of affected people report financial hardship. WHO's linked breast-cancer survival study estimated median 5-year survival at 41.9% in low-income countries versus 87.3% in high-income countries.

This is a direct cancer-care delivery and equity signal rather than a drug-development headline. It quantifies how much avoidable cancer harm now depends on diagnosis pathways, UHC design, workforce, medicine access and social protection rather than on scientific novelty alone.
Health ministries, UHC planners, hospital networks, prevention programs, civil-society advocates, and patients and families facing access or affordability barriers.

Whether countries accelerate cancer inclusion in UHC packages, strengthen prevention and early diagnosis, expand essential-medicine access, and incorporate patient and caregiver burden into cancer policy.
High for a systems signal: the trigger is an official WHO/IARC global report, supported by a same-day peer-reviewed Nature Medicine analysis of breast-cancer survival estimates.

Highly relevant for India because the report centers UHC inclusion, prevention, diagnostics, medicine availability and patient financial strain, but this pass did not verify a new India-specific national survival estimate, reimbursement action, or procurement announcement.
Not applicable. The signal is about affordability and system access rather than a single therapy price.

This is a systems report built from modeled global estimates and uneven underlying data, not an intervention trial. The linked breast-cancer survival paper had observed registry survival data for 67 of 194 WHO Member States, with especially sparse coverage in low-income and fragile settings.
Track country-level policy responses, any WHO implementation follow-up, and newly released country-specific survival or access updates that can sharpen India-relevant comparisons.
Cross-checked across the 8 July 2026 WHO news release, the WHO Global Status Report on Cancer 2026, and the same-day Nature Medicine paper on global breast-cancer survival.

sections: Global oncology systems, especially countries with diagnostic and nursing shortages
2025 baseline capacity vs 2050 projected incidence and staffing needs

Conference coverage reported a 21% rise in cancer incidence by 2050, more than 35 million annual cases, and a projected 100 million cancer-care worker shortfall, including about 65 million nurses and 16 million diagnostic staff.
Therapeutic progress is not enough if systems cannot diagnose, staff infusion units, read scans, deliver radiotherapy or provide palliative care. This directly affects real-world access and survival.
Health ministries, hospital networks, pathology/radiology training pipelines, and patients in both LMIC and high-income settings.
Whether countries invest earlier in workforce planning, task-shifting, digital tools and diagnostic capacity instead of treating access failures as downstream surprises.
Large-scale system modeling and commission-style analysis with direct care-delivery relevance.
Highly relevant for oncology manpower, pathology, radiology and geographic access planning.

This is a projection, not an intervention trial. The full Lancet text was not retrieved in this pass, so country-level assumptions and model sensitivity need direct review before more granular claims are made.
Retrieve the full report and map implications to India-specific workforce and infrastructure gaps.
Conference coverage linked the finding to a Lancet report, but full primary-text capture remains pending.
